title: Linear Programming and Constrained Optimization · Worked Case: Graphical Linear Programming
summary: Choose a fair production and distribution plan under limited resources. Planned artifact: Add and verify the math int graphical linear programming increment in an explainable constrained optimization model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Solve two-variable linear programs graphically and verify corner candidates.
Define decision variables, objective, constraints, units, and feasible domain.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-intermediate cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

steps.title: Calculate Graphical Linear Programming evidence
steps.why: A numerical result is not complete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.

content.text: For a 2×2 matrix, determinant = ad − bc. Use (5 × 8) − (1 × 2).

content.text: Predict the sign and approximate magnitude. Enter the requested quantity with a unit, then compare it with that prediction.
